perturbation.original_text: Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that it is the policy of the Buyer to maintain as secret and confidential all Confidential Information, and the parties hereto recognize that Seller and Guarantor have acquired Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that it is the policy of the Client to maintain as secret and confidential all Confidential Information, and the parties hereto recognize that Seller and Guarantor have acquired Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Buyer' to 'Client' in Section 3, it is unclear whose policy regarding Confidential Information is being referenced. This introduces ambiguity because 'Client' is not defined in the agreement and introduces confusion as to who the parties have a relationship with. This shift contradicts the established understanding that the Buyer is the recipient of the Confidential Information and can make the section non enforceable because of confusion, leading to an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that it is the policy of the Client to maintain as secret and confidential all Confidential Information, and the parties hereto recognize that Seller and Guarantor have acquired Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Buyer' to 'Client' introduces ambiguity as to whose policy is being referenced, directly contradicting the established understanding and potentially making the section unenforceable. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text and the explanation states that Client is not defined anywhere, and 'Buyer' is the main focus of the original contract. Therefore, there is an in-text contradiction as a result of the change, causing a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (d) " Restricted Business " means the Business of the Seller, including all services performed by or on behalf of the Seller for its customers.
perturbation.changed_text: (d) " Restricted Business " means the Business of the Vendor, including all services performed by or on behalf of the Vendor for its customers.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Seller' to 'Vendor' in the definition of "Restricted Business" creates ambiguity. Throughout the document, 'Seller' refers to Accurel Systems International Corporation, whereas 'Vendor' is undefined. By substituting this term, the definition becomes unclear, thereby leading to an in-text contradiction and affecting the scope and enforceability of the non-compete clause described in section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not: (a) directly or indirectly engage in, be employed by, own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or participate in the ownership, management or control of, or otherwise have an interest (whether, subject to Section 5, as a stockholder, director, officer, employee, representative, subcontractor, partner, consultant, proprietor, agent or otherwise) in, or cause, authorize, aid or assist any other person to own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or otherwise have an interest in, any business or any person who is engaged in any business that directly or indirectly competes or intends to compete with the Restricted Business anywhere in the world, unless Seller or Guarantor purchase or own less than five percent (5%) of capital stock in a publicly held company; or (b) directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for itself or on behalf of any other person, canvass or solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be solicited, or enter into or effect, or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be entered into or effected, any business or orders for businesses competing with the Restricted Business from any person who (i) at the time of the Agreement or within two years prior to the date of the Agreement, has been, a customer or client, or (ii) is an active prospect to be a customer or client, in each case, of the Seller at the time of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Seller' to 'Vendor' in the definition of "Restricted Business" creates a contradiction. The contradicted text refers to 'Seller' when outlining restrictions related to the Restricted Business. Since 'Seller' is explicitly defined as Accurel Systems International Corporation, replacing it with the undefined term 'Vendor' creates ambiguity and uncertainty, making it unclear who is bound by the restrictions outlined in the contradicted text. This directly impacts the enforceability of the non-compete clause, thus causing a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that any breach or threatened breach by it of any of the covenants or provisions contained herein will result in irreparable and continuing harm to the Buyer for which the Buyer would not have adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that any breach or threatened breach by it of any of the covenants or provisions contained herein will result in irreparable and continuing harm to the Recipient for which the Buyer would not have adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.explanation: Introducing 'Recipient' alongside 'Buyer' in Section 5 creates uncertainty about who is entitled to protection from breaches. 'Recipient' is undefined, making it ambiguous who can claim irreparable harm, which contradicts the section's intention to protect the 'Buyer'. This creates an in-text contradiction due to the introduction of this new term, without being previously defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Right to Injunctive Relief. Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that any breach or threatened breach by it of any of the covenants or provisions contained herein will result in irreparable and continuing harm to the Recipient for which the Buyer would not have adequate remedy at law. Therefore, Seller and Guarantor acknowledges and agrees that, in addition to any other remedy which the Buyer may have at law or in equity, the Buyer shall be entitled to injunctive relief or other equitable remedies in the event of any such breach or threatened breach. Seller and Guarantor further acknowledges and agrees that monetary damages would be insufficient to compensate the Buyer in the event of a breach by Seller or Guarantor of any of the covenants or provisions contained herein, and that in the event of a breach thereof, the Buyer shall be entitled to specific performance of the obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'Recipient' alongside 'Buyer,' creating uncertainty about who is entitled to protection from breaches. The contradicted text explicitly states that the 'Buyer' is entitled to injunctive relief and specific performance, indicating that the 'Buyer' is the intended beneficiary of the covenants. The changed text adds ambiguity by including 'Recipient,' which is not defined and thus contradicts the clear intention in the contradicted text, therefore creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not, directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for themselves or on behalf of any other person: (a) solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause to be solicited for employment, any persons who (i) are, at the time of solicitation of employment, employees of the Seller, Buyer or any of their respective Affiliates, or (ii) are, at the time of solicitation of employment, sales representatives or employees thereof, retained by the Buyer or any of its Affiliates
perturbation.changed_text: At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not, directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for themselves or on behalf of any other person: (a) solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause to be solicited for employment, any persons who (i) are, at the time of solicitation of employment, employees of the Contractor, Buyer or any of their respective Affiliates, or (ii) are, at the time of solicitation of employment, sales representatives or employees thereof, retained by the Buyer or any of its Affiliates
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the word Seller with the word Contractor within section 4, subsection a, we are introducing a conflict. The new term 'Contractor' may or may not be an Affilate of the seller. The original text states that the seller and Guarantor, shall not solicit employees for the seller, Buyer or any of their respective Affiliates. By substituting 'Contractor' for 'Seller' within the paragraph, we are creating confusion in who the paragraph is refering to. This has created an in-text contradiction, which may or may not make the contract difficult to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Solicitation. At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not, directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for themselves or on behalf of any other person: (a) solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause to be solicited for employment, any persons who (i) are, at the time of solicitation of employment, employees of the Contractor, Buyer or any of their respective Affiliates, or (ii) are, at the time of solicitation of employment, sales representatives or employees thereof, retained by the Buyer or any of its Affiliates; or (b) employ or, directly or indirectly, cause to be employed, any persons who (i) are, at the time of such action, employees of the Buyer or any of its Affiliates, or (ii) are, at the time of such action, sales representatives or employees thereof, retained by the Buyer or any of its Affiliates; provided, however, that this Section 4 shall not prohibit Seller or Guarantor from employing or soliciting the employment any person who (A) is an employee of Seller as of the Agreement Date and (B) is not offered employment by Buyer as of the Agreement Date.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Seller and Guarantor shall not solicit employees for the Seller, Buyer or any of their respective Affiliates'. The changed text replaces 'Seller' with 'Contractor'. The Contradicted Text also states 'Seller and Guarantor shall not solicit employees for the Contractor, Buyer or any of their respective Affiliates'. The Perturbation Explanation states that this change creates a conflict because the term 'Contractor' may or may not be an Affiliate of the seller. Therefore, the change causes a strong, clear contradiction by introducing a different entity and potentially broadening the scope of the non-solicitation agreement, creating uncertainty and misalignment in terminology according to the definitions, thus leading to in-text contradiction.
